channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Australian Involvement in US-Iran Tensions Raises Concerns Over International Law Compliance
item.description: The Albanese government is facing criticism over Australia's potential complicity in US-led military actions against Iran, following the sinking of an Iranian ship off Sri Lanka by a US submarine. Rep...

item.title: Challenges in Tanzania’s Microfinance Sector Affect Lending Capacity
item.description: Tanzania's microfinance sector, wey long time serve as main help for small businesses, farmers, and low-income borrowers, inah face serious wahala with cash availability, government rules, and loan re...

item.title: Ian Huntley, convicted Soham murderer, dies after attack in prison
item.description: Ian Huntley, the convicted murderer of 10-year-olds Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman, has died in hospital following a violent attack in prison. The 52-year-old was found in a pool of blood on 26 Febru...

item.title: Joeboy talks about challenges new artists face in breaking into the music industry
item.description: Afrobeats singer Joeboy don raise alarm on how e hard for new artists wey wan make name for music industry now. For recent interview with Jay On Air, e talk say the wahala don increase mainly because ...

item.title: Rawalpindi ATC convicts 47 PTI leaders and supporters in GHQ attack case
item.description: RAWALPINDI: An anti-terrorism court in Rawalpindi on Saturday handed down 10-year prison sentences to 47 PTI leaders and supporters over their alleged involvement in the May 9, 2023 GHQ attack. The co...

item.title: Boity Thulo collaborates with Bathu on new sneaker collection 'The Volt Pack'
item.description: South African star Boity Thulo has added another venture to her multifaceted career, unveiling 'The Volt Pack', a new sneaker collection created in collaboration with local brand Bathu. Known for her ...

item.title: Bong Suntay’s Remarks and Reflections on Political Behavior in the Philippines
item.description: The article discusses Quezon City Representative Bong Suntay’s controversial remarks about celebrity Anne Curtis, comparing his behavior to that seen during the Duterte administration. The writer argu...

item.title: Mitchell Santner confident New Zealand can challenge India in T20 World Cup final
item.description: As the ICC Men's T20 World Cup final approaches on Sunday, March 8, 2026, New Zealand captain Mitchell Santner spoke to the media about his team's mindset ahead of the crucial match against India at t...

item.title: B.C. girl critically injured in Tumbler Ridge shooting begins to breathe independently
item.description: The mother of 12-year-old Maya Gebala, critically injured in the Tumbler Ridge, B.C., mass shooting last month, has shared that her daughter’s breathing tube has been removed as doctors monitor her ab...

item.title: TS Galaxy assistant coach emphasises caution ahead of Nedbank Cup clash with Jacksa Spears
item.description: TS Galaxy assistant coach Bernard Parker is approaching the Nedbank Cup quarterfinal against lower-division side Jacksa Spears with caution. Parker, who has first-hand experience of cup upsets, was pa...

item.title: Nairobi Floods Highlight Need for Stronger Drainage and Water Harvesting Systems
item.description: Following the recent heavy rains, Nairobi experienced widespread flooding that affected key roads such as Mombasa Road, Kenyatta Avenue, Thika Road, Dunga Road, and Enterprise Road. Residential estate...

item.title: DOH reminds public to stay alert on rabies as cases drop in 2026
item.description: The Department of Health (DOH) in the Philippines reminded the public to remain vigilant against rabies despite a significant decrease in reported cases during the first two months of 2026. From Janua...

item.title: Escalation in Middle East conflict: Iran launches retaliatory missile strikes on multiple countries as Israel and US intensify attacks on Tehran
item.description: The ongoing war in the Middle East has entered its eighth day, with Iran launching a series of retaliatory missile and drone attacks across the region in response to joint US-Israeli strikes that kill...

item.title: 47 PTI leaders convicted for organising May 9 riots, fined and properties confiscated
item.description: RAWALPINDI: On Saturday, an Anti-Terrorism Court (ATC) in Rawalpindi handed down 10-year jail terms to 47 individuals, including prominent Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (PTI) leaders such as Omar Ayub, Zar...

item.title: Brumbies aim to continue unbeaten start against Reds despite lightning delay
item.description: The ACT Brumbies have kicked off the Super Rugby Pacific season in perfect fashion, securing wins in their opening fixtures. Their next challenge is against the Queensland Reds at Canberra Stadium, al...

item.title: Dubai Airport Resumes Partial Operations After Temporary Suspension Amid Israel-Iran Tensions
item.description: Dubai International Airport, the world’s busiest hub for international flights, briefly suspended all operations on the morning of March 7, 2026, following an aerial interception in the vicinity amid ...

item.title: Truck Loaded with Cattle Crashes in Baguio, Killing Driver and Passenger, Injuring Minor
item.description: In Baguio City, a tragic road accident occurred yesterday afternoon when a truck transporting cattle lost control in Barangay Asin. The vehicle collided with a Toyota Innova, smashed through a series ...

item.title: Security forces neutralise 15 terrorists in Balochistan during intelligence operations
item.description: Security forces in Pakistan successfully conducted intelligence-based operations (IBOs) in Balochistan, eliminating a total of 15 terrorists linked to India. According to the Inter-Services Public Rel...

item.title: Analysis of Potential Challenges Facing Prime Minister Mark Carney
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney, approaching the one-year mark in office, is currently enjoying strong polling support with the Liberals at 49 per cent nationwide, while the Conservatives sit at 35 per cen...

item.title: Michael Milton's family set to fly to Italy after delays in Doha due to Middle East conflict
item.description: Michael Milton, Australia's most decorated Winter Paralympian, is preparing to compete in his sixth Paralympics at Milano-Cortina after recovering from a broken leg. Meanwhile, his wife Penni and teen...
